subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the story set in a space station and the role of Claribel the canary
Learn how observation and quick thinking can help detect dangers
Identify the main characters such as Sven Olsen and the narrator
Understand how animals can help humans in scientific situations
Recognize the importance of safety systems in space stations
Develop reading comprehension and vocabulary skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Feathered Friends
topics_covered: Different types of birds kept as pets
Discussion about animals in unusual environments like space
teaching_strategy: Class discussion about birds and pets
Question–answer session from the pre-reading activity
Teacher introduction to the story setting
student_activities: Discuss questions about feathered and furry friends
Share favourite birds and reasons for liking them

lesson_plan.title: Background of the Story
topics_covered: Role of astronauts and construction workers in space
Introduction to Sven Olsen and Claribel
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of space station environment
Reading the beginning of the story aloud
student_activities: Read the opening paragraphs of the story

lesson_plan.title: Understanding the Main Events
topics_covered: Claribel the canary in the space station
How Claribel adapts to zero gravity
Crew members’ reactions to the bird
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the setting and events
student_activities: Describe how Claribel moves in zero gravity
Discuss why Sven brought the bird to the station

lesson_plan.title: The Air Problem and Discovery
topics_covered: Realization that something is wrong with the air
Connection with canaries used in coal mines
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the turning point in the story
Discussion about how animals can detect danger
student_activities: Explain why the crew suspected an air problem
Discuss the historical use of canaries in mines

lesson_plan.title: Resolution and Message
topics_covered: Discovery of the air purifier failure
How the crew fixed the problem
Importance of observation and quick thinking
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the ending of the story
Discussion on the message of the story
student_activities: Write what lesson the story teaches
Share examples of animals helping humans

lesson_plan.title: Language and Revision Activities
topics_covered: Merged words such as brunch, smog, and motel
student_activities: Match words to form merged words
Complete sentences using future tense forms

teaching_aids: Chart explaining canaries used in coal mines
teacher_tip: Encourage students to discuss how Claribel helped detect the air problem. Connecting the story with the real-life practice of using canaries in coal mines will help students understand the scientific idea behind the story.
